objectives: Explain core TCP/IP concepts (IP addressing, ports, protocols) and how they relate to security monitoring and investigations
Interpret basic network flows to distinguish normal vs suspicious behavior
Apply networking fundamentals to support later log analysis and threat detection tasks
learning_path.title: TCP/IP and Networking Foundations for Security Work

objectives: Describe key network security concepts and where controls are applied in typical environments
Relate network security controls to observable telemetry used in monitoring and investigations
Identify common network-based attack patterns at a conceptual level
learning_path.title: Network Security Fundamentals and Common Control Points

objectives: Explain what logs are, why they matter, and common log fields used in investigations
Perform basic log triage to identify anomalies and relevant events
Correlate simple events across multiple log sources to build a timeline
learning_path.title: Log Analysis Essentials

objectives: Describe how a SIEM supports security monitoring workflows
Run basic searches/queries to retrieve relevant events for an alert
Triage alerts by validating evidence and documenting findings
learning_path.title: Security Monitoring with SIEM: Ingestion, Queries, and Alert Triage

objectives: Differentiate between indicators, behaviors, and hypotheses in threat detection
Analyze event patterns to assess likelihood and potential impact
Document an analysis narrative that supports next investigative steps
learning_path.title: Threat Detection and Analysis: From Signals to Hypotheses

objectives: Explain common endpoint telemetry types and how they support investigations
Interpret endpoint alerts and validate them with supporting evidence
Select appropriate endpoint containment or remediation actions based on findings
learning_path.title: Endpoint Security (EDR/AV): Telemetry, Detections, and Response Actions

objectives: Explain core Windows and Active Directory security concepts relevant to monitoring and investigations
Identify high-value Windows/AD audit events and how they appear in logs
Analyze common identity-related attack patterns using Windows/AD telemetry
learning_path.title: Windows Security and Active Directory: Identity, Authentication, and Audit Signals

objectives: Explain key Linux security concepts relevant to operational monitoring
Identify common Linux log sources and interpret security-relevant events
Analyze suspicious activity patterns on Linux systems using available telemetry
learning_path.title: Linux Security: Accounts, Services, and Audit Evidence

objectives: Explain the vulnerability management lifecycle and stakeholder needs
Prioritize vulnerabilities using risk context and operational constraints
Translate vulnerability findings into actionable remediation tracking
learning_path.title: Vulnerability Management: Prioritization and Operational Workflow

objectives: Execute a structured incident response workflow from initial report to closure
Build an incident timeline using evidence from monitoring and endpoint sources
Define containment and eradication steps aligned to observed attacker behavior
learning_path.title: Incident Response: Triage, Containment, Eradication, and Recovery

objectives: Install, configure, and run open source software in a controlled environment
Validate correct operation through basic checks and output inspection
Apply safe operational practices when using open source tools in workflows
learning_path.title: Operating Open Source Software for Security Operations

objectives: Navigate an application-specific interface to complete routine operational tasks
Use interface features to retrieve, filter, and review relevant information
Document repeatable steps for consistent execution of tasks in the interface
learning_path.title: Using an Application-Specific Interface for Security Workflows

objectives: Formulate clear questions and hypotheses based on observed security signals
Design a simple methodology to test hypotheses using available evidence
Communicate findings with appropriate limitations and reproducibility
learning_path.title: Scientific Research Methodology for Security Investigations

objectives: Explain how data science supports detection, analysis, and prioritization tasks
Perform basic exploratory analysis to identify patterns and outliers in security-relevant data
Translate analytical results into operationally useful conclusions
learning_path.title: Data Science for Security Analytics

objectives: Assess current capability against role expectations and identify gaps
Create a structured development plan with measurable milestones
Track progress and adjust the plan based on outcomes and feedback
learning_path.title: Managing Personal Professional Development for Security Roles

matched_occupation.description: Computer scientists conduct research in computer and information science, directed toward greater knowledge and understanding of fundamental aspects of ICT phenomena. They write research reports and proposals. Computer scientists also invent and design new approaches to computing technology, find innovative uses for existing technology and studies and solve complex problems in computing.
matched_occupation.label: computer scientist
